skill: Ratios, rates, proportional relationships, and units
stem: A long-distance cyclist completed a leg of a tour with an average speed of 22.500 miles per hour. What was this average speed, in yards per hour? (1 mile = 1,760 yards)
rationale: The correct answer is 39,600. Since 1 mile = 1,760 yards, 22.500 miles per hour is equivalent to 22.500 · 1,760 = 39,600 yards per hour.
